title: Research
text:
Research




My research focuses on tightly coupled cooperative mobile multi-robot systems that can scale with task size. In these systems, several robots handle a common object and coordinate motion in scenarios that cannot be solved by a single robot alone.

title: Research
text:
Core questions

How can multiple mobile robots handle a common object without relying on highly specialized hardware?
How can cooperative robot systems scale from small to large configurations while remaining easy to program?
How can mobile manipulators handle large-scale components despite localization errors, communication latency, and kinematic overdetermination?
How can robotic automation become more adaptable for changing products, component sizes, and production environments?
How can reusable robotic teams improve utilization when the task spectrum is too broad for dedicated special-purpose machines?

title: Research
text:
Research themes



Cooperative mobile multi-robot systems


I investigate cooperative mobile multi-robot systems in which several robots work on the same physical object. This creates strong spatial and temporal dependencies between the robots and introduces challenges such as synchronization, localization accuracy, communication latency, and kinematic overdetermination.




Scalable control and path planning


A central goal of my work is to make cooperative robot systems scalable. Adding or removing robots should not require redesigning the entire system. My research combines formation path planning, decentralized control, and hardware-independent system architectures.




Mobile manipulation for industrial automation


I work on mobile manipulators for large-scale object transport, handling, and assembly. These systems are particularly relevant when components are too large, too heavy, or too variable for conventional single-robot solutions.




Construction automation and mobile additive manufacturing


Beyond industrial handling and assembly, I work on mobile robotic systems for construction automation, including mobile additive manufacturing and print-while-drive processes.

title: Research
text:
Approach



System perspective


I am interested in the complete robotic system, including mobile platforms, manipulators, tools, sensors, controllers, path planners, communication, and experimental validation. The goal is not only to design algorithms, but to make them work on real robotic hardware.




Experimental validation


My work emphasizes physical experiments with mobile robots and mobile manipulators. This includes cooperative object transport, cooperative object handling, and mobile additive manufacturing in laboratory and production-like environments.

title: Projects
text:
Projects




Selected projects that show the system-level focus of my work: cooperative transport, cooperative object handling, and mobile additive manufacturing.

title: Projects
text:
Cooperative object handling
Cooperative object handling extends multi-robot cooperation from planar transport to spatial manipulation. Multiple mobile manipulators jointly grasp and move one object in up to six degrees of freedom.
This project focuses on the control challenges that arise when several manipulators are mechanically coupled through the same object, including overdetermination, force load, workspace limitations, and adaptation to different object geometries.
Key aspects

Multi-manipulator object handling
Admittance-based and compliance-oriented control
Handling of kinematic overdetermination
Adaptation to different object sizes, shapes, and weights
Experiments with up to eight manipulators


Video CASE 2025 paper Dissertation

title: Projects
text:
Cooperative object transport
Cooperative object transport investigates how multiple mobile robot platforms can jointly move large-scale objects through industrial environments.
The work focuses on scalable formation control, curvature-aware path planning, and the evaluation of different formation geometries such as serial, parallel, and arbitrary formations.
Key aspects

Formation control for nonholonomic mobile robots
Cooperative transport of large-scale objects
Path-curvature constraints
Object slippage and tracking error evaluation
Scalability across different robot formations


Video Dissertation Related publications

title: Projects
text:
Print-while-drive additive manufacturing
This project investigates trajectory planning for mobile manipulators that perform additive manufacturing while the mobile platform is moving.
Instead of separating driving and printing into discrete steps, the robot continuously coordinates platform motion and manipulator motion to extend the reachable workspace and enable large-scale additive manufacturing processes.
Key aspects

Mobile additive manufacturing
Offline platform trajectory planning
Coordinated platform-manipulator motion
Construction automation
Large-scale robotic fabrication


Video CASE 2025 paper Related publications

title: Home
text:
Research profile




Postdoctoral Researcher · Institute of Assembly Technology and Robotics · Leibniz University Hannover


I develop scalable cooperative mobile multi-robot systems for industrial handling, assembly, and construction automation.

I design control, trajectory planning, and system integration approaches for tightly coupled robotic cooperation. My focus is on tasks where multiple mobile robots handle one common object and where no individual robot could complete the task alone, even given unlimited time.

Explore research View projects Download dissertation

title: Home
text:
Research focus

My work is centered on tightly coupled cooperation between mobile robots. I focus on scenarios where several robots handle one common object and coordinate their motion so closely that the task only exists as a team task. This is especially relevant for broad task spectra in which highly specialized robots would be expensive, underutilized, and difficult to justify.



Cooperative mobile multi-robot systems
Scalable control frameworks for mobile robots and mobile manipulators that jointly transport, handle, and assemble large-scale components.


Mobile manipulation and control
Coordination of mobile platforms and robotic arms, including formation control, trajectory planning, and whole-body control.


Automation for construction and production
Robotic automation for flexible manufacturing, large-scale assembly, and mobile additive manufacturing.

title: Dissertation
text:
Dissertation




A scalable CMMRS framework for collaborative object handling and assembly tasks in industrial settings.

title: Dissertation
text:
Summary
My dissertation investigates the design, development, and implementation of a scalable Cooperative Mobile Multi-Robot System (CMMRS) framework for collaborative object handling and assembly tasks in industrial settings.
The work develops a hardware-agnostic control structure that allows multiple mobile robots and mobile manipulators to operate either independently or cooperatively. It combines formation path planning, decentralized control, manipulator control, and whole-body coordination to improve scalability and adaptability in industrial automation.
The system was experimentally evaluated across object transport, cooperative object handling, and assembly-related scenarios, including configurations with up to eight robots.

title: Dissertation
text:
Core contributions



Scalable CMMRS framework


A framework for scaling and adapting cooperative mobile multi-robot systems to changing product and process parameters.




Formation path planning and control


Path planning and control approaches for cooperative object transport with nonholonomic mobile platforms.




Cooperative object handling


Control methods for multiple mobile manipulators that jointly grasp and move large-scale components.




Robust system integration


Design choices that reduce sensitivity to communication latency, localization uncertainty, and hardware-specific features.

title: Dissertation
text:
Application domains







Domain
Description




Object transport
Large-scale objects are transported by several cooperating mobile platforms.


Object handling
Multiple manipulators move one object in a coordinated way, including spatial motion.


Cooperative assembly
Handling-related tasks with higher accuracy requirements and contact forces.

title: Privacy
text:
Privacy




This site is designed as a small, static, privacy-friendly research profile.

title: Privacy
text:
Technical choices

No analytics are included.
No cookies are intentionally set by this website.
Images and the dissertation PDF are hosted locally with the site.
YouTube videos are not embedded. Project pages use local thumbnails and open YouTube only after an explicit click.
No external fonts are loaded.

title: Privacy
text:
External links
The site links to external services such as GitHub, ORCID, LinkedIn, Scopus, the institute website, DOI pages, and YouTube. These external sites may process data according to their own privacy policies.
